nodes.name: Citrine warbler
nodes.summary: The citrine warbler (Myiothlypis luteoviridis) is a species of bird in the family Parulidae. The term citrine refers to its yellowish coloration. It is found in Bolivia, Colombia, Ecuador, Peru, and Venezuela. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist montane forests. Behaviour Pairs of citrine warblers are known to forage in the lower levels and edges of forests. They often do this among mixed-species flocks. References
